records.ety: [L., fr. res, gen. trium, three + vir a man. See Three, and Virile.]

defs: One of tree men united in public office or authority.
notes: ☞ In later times the triumvirs of Rome were three men who jointly exercised sovereign power. Julius Caesar, Crassus, and Pompey were the first triumvirs; Octavianus (Augustus), Antony, and Lepidus were the second and last.
